title: Shivering Thermogenesis Explained
script_summary: An insightful dive into shivering thermogenesis and its role in body heat production.

segments.voiceover: Did you know shivering can help keep you warm?
It's called shivering thermogenesis—our body’s natural response to cold.
When we’re cold, tiny muscles contract, generating heat. But how does that work?
This process activates our brown fat, a special type of fat that burns energy.
So, shivering isn’t just annoying; it’s vital for survival in the cold.
Curious about how your body adapts? Hit follow for more science insights!
